title: bassia paradoxa
intro: Bassia paradoxa, also known as dodder, is a perennial parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to and drawing them from its host plant. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Bassia paradoxa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Bassia paradoxa are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to soothe sore throats and ease respiratory discomfort.
